mnemonics: JVM Cleans Garbage: Just Vacate Memory by Collecting Garbage
keyPoints: Garbage Collection is the automatic memory management process in Java that reclaims memory used by objects that are no longer reachable.
An object becomes eligible for garbage collection when there are no more references to it.
The finalize() method is called by the garbage collector before an object is destroyed, but its execution is not guaranteed.
commonMistakes: Manually trying to force garbage collection with System.gc() - it's only a suggestion to the JVM, not a command.
Relying on the finalize() method to release critical resources like file handles or database connections, as it may not be called promptly or at all.
